summary: Reference matrix for type casts, prototype misuse, and const assignment rules.

cases.upstream_intent: Explicit conversions should succeed when the target surface allows them.
cases.zr_contract: Explicit casts are the public surface for numeric conversion and do not rely on implicit narrowing.
cases.expected_outcome: supported explicit casts compile and execute
assertions: AST encodes an explicit cast node
compiler records the target type
runtime result matches converted value

aot_c.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove native AOT C execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=aot_c

aot_llvm.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove native AOT LLVM execution when the LLVM host toolchain is present; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=aot_llvm

return: supported explicit casts compile and execute

cases.upstream_intent: Type-directed construction and casting need a positive baseline for supported prototype paths.
cases.zr_contract: Supported prototype-backed casts use explicit type identity rather than name-based fallbacks.
cases.expected_outcome: supported prototype or value casts preserve target identity
assertions: target type metadata is recorded
no stringly type-name fallback is used
runtime type reflection reports the target type

cases.upstream_intent: Misusing construction syntax should fail as a type rule, not later at runtime.

type checker reports the target identity
artifact text omits a constructor path

cases.upstream_intent: Numeric narrowing needs explicit boundary tests near truncation limits.
cases.zr_contract: Narrowing conversions require explicit casts and expose deterministic boundary behavior.
cases.expected_outcome: boundary numeric casts behave consistently at min and max values
assertions: boundary values are enumerated explicitly
overflow or truncation rules are stable
artifact records the explicit cast op

cases.upstream_intent: Immutable parameter rules should align with the same diagnostic family as const locals.

assertions: diagnostic family is shared across local and parameter bindings
project compile path reproduces the same rule
artifact generation stops before code emission

cases.upstream_intent: Cast semantics and default expressions need a combined regression point.
cases.zr_contract: Explicit casts inside default expressions are type-checked before call lowering.
cases.expected_outcome: default expressions containing casts preserve type correctness and runtime value
assertions: default expression AST retains the cast node
compiler type-checks the default eagerly
runtime call result matches the defaulted cast value

project fixture compiles through module boundaries

cases.upstream_intent: Prototype misuse often regresses when new construction forms are added.
cases.zr_contract: Prototype misuse is rejected before runtime and never falls back to a dynamic map or member lookup path.
cases.expected_outcome: prototype misuse remains a compile-time failure after constructor refactors
assertions: compiler reports prototype identity in the error
runtime never sees a bogus constructor call
artifact omits fallback lookup ops

assertions: field metadata marks immutability explicitly
runtime never applies a hidden mutable write

cases.upstream_intent: Strongly typed references prefer explicit narrowing over silent coercion.
cases.zr_contract: ZR does not allow silent narrowing conversions where the language contract requires an explicit cast.
cases.expected_outcome: implicit narrowing attempts fail with a targeted type diagnostic
assertions: diagnostic suggests an explicit cast
runtime is never reached for the bad assignment
artifact omits an implicit conversion op

aot_c.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove native AOT C execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=aot_c

cases.upstream_intent: Dynamic truthiness does not imply universal cast permissiveness in ZR.
cases.zr_contract: ZR does not auto-import Python-style truthiness or arbitrary object-to-bool casting without an explicit contract.
cases.expected_outcome: object-to-bool coercion without an explicit contract is rejected
assertions: type checker demands an explicit supported conversion
runtime contract remains explicit and typed
project fixture diagnostics match the same rule

aot_c.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove native AOT C execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=aot_c
